decks.notes: The main focus of this deck is to assemble the Greasefang Parhelion combo, utilizing cards like Parhelion II and Greasefang, Okiba Boss to overwhelm opponents. The deck also includes various support cards like Fatal Push and Thoughtseize to control the game and disrupt the opponent's strategy. With a mix of creatures, removal spells, and lands that provide utility, the deck aims to create a powerful board state that can quickly close out the game through the combo or by outvaluing the opponent.

The deck focuses on controlling the opponent's actions through a combination of removal spells, hand disruption, and card draw. It also utilizes Yorion, Sky Nomad as a companion to provide additional value by blinking permanents for extra card advantage. The deck aims to outvalue the opponent and control the game until it can win with its finishers, such as Kaito, Bane of Nightmares and Stormchaser's Talent. Additionally, it includes cards that can bounce or exile problematic permanents, such as Boomerang Basics and Hopeless Nightmare, to further disrupt the opponent's game plan.

The deck focuses on disrupting the opponent's strategy and controlling the game with removal spells and hand disruption. It also utilizes mana acceleration with Llanowar Elves and Mutavault to cast powerful threats like Sheoldred, the Apocalypse and Sentinel of the Nameless City. The deck aims to maintain board presence and resource advantage through efficient removal and card advantage. The inclusion of Boseiju, Who Endures and Dark Depths provides additional utility and flexibility in handling various matchups. Overall, the deck aims to outvalue and outlast the opponent with efficient control elements.

This deck focuses on quickly deploying low-cost aggressive creatures and burning spells to quickly deal damage to the opponent. By utilizing efficient creatures like Monastery Swiftspear and Soul-Scar Mage, along with burn spells like Fiery Impulse and Reckless Rage, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent early in the game. The deck also includes cards like Academic Dispute and Boomerang Basics to disrupt the opponent's strategy and maintain control of the battlefield. The key strategy is to apply constant pressure and finish off the opponent before they have a chance to stabilize.

The main objective of this deck is to assemble the Lotus Field combo as quickly as possible to generate a large amount of mana. This is achieved by playing cards like Arboreal Grazer and Port of Karfell to ramp up mana, before using cards like Scapeshift and Spelunking to find and play Lotus Field. Once Lotus Field is on the battlefield, the player can generate a large amount of mana to cast high impact spells like Morlun, Devourer of Spiders or Formidable Speaker to quickly overwhelm the opponent. The deck aims to win the game through this explosive mana generation and powerful spell casting.

The main concept of this deck is to overwhelm the opponent with aggressive and fast-paced red creatures and burn spells. The strategy revolves around dealing damage quickly and efficiently, using cards like Soul-Scar Mage, Monastery Swiftspear, and Burst Lightning to apply pressure. The deck also includes utility lands like Den of the Bugbear and Ramunap Ruins to provide added versatility. Overall, the deck aims to be proactive and explosive in order to secure a victory before the opponent can stabilize.

The main focus of this deck is to overwhelm the opponent with aggressive early game creatures and efficient burn spells to quickly reduce their life total to zero. The deck aims to maintain pressure on the opponent with creatures like Monastery Swiftspear and Soul-Scar Mage, while also having access to powerful burn spells like Burst Lightning and Lightning Strike to finish off the opponent or clear the way for your creatures to attack. Additionally, the inclusion of cards like Monstrous Rage and Reckless Rage provide additional ways to push damage through and maintain board control. The goal is to close out the game quickly before the opponent can stabilize or mount a comeback.

This deck focuses on using a combination of creature removal, hand disruption, and efficient creatures to control the board and outvalue the opponent. By utilizing cards like Abrupt Decay, Fatal Push, and Thoughtseize to disrupt the opponent's game plan, while deploying threats like Badgermole Cub, Ouroboroid, and Sentinel of the Nameless City to pressure the opponent, this deck aims to grind out incremental advantages and win the game through attrition. With a mix of removal, disruption, and threats, this deck looks to disrupt the opponent's strategy and establish control of the game.

The main concept of this UR Aggro deck is to overwhelm the opponent with fast and efficient creatures while using spells like Reckless Rage and Monstrous Rage to clear the way for attacks. The deck also includes cards like Academic Dispute and Boomerang Basics to disrupt the opponent's game plan. With a focus on low-cost creatures like Monastery Swiftspear and Soul-Scar Mage, as well as additional burn spells like Emberheart Challenger, the deck aims to win quickly through aggressive plays and powerful synergies.

The deck focuses on aggressive play with the use of cheap, efficient creatures and burn spells to quickly deal damage to the opponent. The key strategy involves using cards like Monastery Swiftspear and Emberheart Challenger to apply early pressure and finish off the opponent with spells like Fiery Impulse and Reckless Rage. The deck also includes some card draw and utility spells like Boomerang Basics and Academic Dispute to maintain consistency and disrupt the opponent's game plan. Overall, the deck aims to win as quickly as possible through aggressive creature damage and burn spells.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly overwhelm the opponent with aggressive creatures and direct damage spells. By utilizing cards like Monastery Swiftspear and Soul-Scar Mage to deal early damage, paired with burst damage spells like Lightning Strike and Burst Lightning, the goal is to reduce the opponent's life total as quickly as possible. Den of the Bugbear and Ramunap Ruins provide additional sources of damage, while cards like Reckless Rage and Monstrous Rage help to clear the opponent's board and push through for lethal damage.

The main concept of this deck is to overwhelm the opponent with aggressive creatures and powerful spells. By utilizing cards like Monastery Swiftspear, Soul-Scar Mage, and Emberheart Challenger, along with instant and sorcery spells like Reckless Rage and Academic Dispute, the deck aims to deal quick and consistent damage to the opponent. The inclusion of cards like Boomerang Basics and Multiversal Passage allows for flexibility in dealing with opposing threats. With a focus on speed and efficiency, this UR Aggro deck looks to take control of the game early and maintain pressure throughout.

This deck focuses on utilizing aggressive creatures and burn spells to quickly deal damage to the opponent. It combines cheap creatures like Monastery Swiftspear and Soul-Scar Mage with powerful instant spells like Burst Lightning and Reckless Rage to put pressure on the opponent early in the game. The key strategy is to maintain a steady stream of threats while disrupting the opponent's game plan with removal spells and bouncing effects. By maintaining a fast-paced, aggressive game plan, this deck aims to overwhelm the opponent before they can stabilize.

The deck focuses on quickly playing and protecting spirits while maintaining card advantage through Enduring Curiosity and Spell Queller. It utilizes powerful spirit cards like Rattlechains, Supreme Phantom, and Skyclave Apparition to pressure the opponent and disrupt their plays. The mana base allows for consistent access to the necessary colors while Mutavault provides an additional threat. The deck's strategy revolves around aggressive spirit creatures backed up by disruptive spells, aiming to overwhelm the opponent and secure victory through efficient and synergistic plays.

The main concept of this deck is to establish control over the game by using disruption spells like Thoughtseize and Duress while also utilizing efficient removal spells like Fatal Push and Go for the Throat. The deck aims to stabilize the board with creatures like Sentinel of the Nameless City and Sheoldred, the Apocalypse, while maintaining card advantage with cards like Unholy Annex / Ritual Chamber. The deck also incorporates mana acceleration with Llanowar Elves and Mutavault to quickly ramp into powerful threats like Sheoldred. Overall, the strategy revolves around controlling the game and overwhelming the opponent with powerful creatures.

The key concept of this Gruul Aggro deck is to overwhelm the opponent with fast, aggressive creatures and burn spells. By utilizing efficient creatures like Emberheart Challenger and Soul-Scar Mage alongside powerful burn spells like Burst Lightning and Reckless Rage, the deck aims to quickly reduce the opponent's life total to zero. Additionally, the inclusion of cards like Questing Druid and Kumano Faces Kakkazan provides additional utility and ways to push through damage. The deck's game plan revolves around applying constant pressure and ending the game before the opponent can stabilize.
